metadata.title: Why the Same AI Tool Helps a Teacher and Harms a Student
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Claude book candidate 06. Mechanism: the phase gate — the specific point at which AI stops and human cognitive work begins — determines whether AI amplifies capability or substitutes for it. Teacher's gate is at lesson delivery; student's gate is at writing and reasoning. Same tool, opposite outcomes because the gate sits in different places.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL (#1F6F5C) = capability building / human cognitive work / kept; CRIMSON (#BF3339) = capability borrowing / delegated cognitive work / lost. Never swap mid-film.
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: NO extended neuroscience of synaptic plasticity, NO specific curriculum reform proposals, NO comparison of particular educational AI platforms.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: A teacher who uses AI to prepare lessons teaches better. A student who uses AI to write the essay learns nothing. Same tool. Opposite outcome.

production_viz.label: same tool — two outcomes
production_viz.mechanic: two columns: left TEACHER uses AI (teal outcome 'BETTER TEACHING'); right STUDENT uses AI (crimson outcome 'LEARNED NOTHING'); same AI chip in the center between them

beats.narration_text: Both used AI for schoolwork. One improved their teaching. The other undermined their learning. The tool is identical. The outcome is reversed.
beats.scene_description: teacher at whiteboard presenting to students, confident and engaged; editorial newsprint desaturated treatment; slow push-in
beats.image_prompt: B02, teacher at a whiteboard presenting to a classroom, confident engaged posture, editorial desaturated newsprint treatment, flat print lighting, no text legible, collage aesthetic

beats.narration_text: A teacher and a student both used the same AI tool for their schoolwork. One improved their teaching. The other undermined their learning. Why?

card.copy: Same tool — one improved, one was harmed. Why?

beats.narration_text: You might expect that AI assistance always helps — less friction means more output, more output means more learning. The tool does the same thing for both: generates high-quality text. The benefit should be the same.

production_viz.label: naive model: AI assistance always helps equally
production_viz.mechanic: two chips 'TEACHER + AI' and 'STUDENT + AI' both pointing to 'BETTER OUTCOME' chip; all teal; labeled 'the naive expectation'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the naive model — AI always helps equally

beats.narration_text: The phase gate is the specific point at which AI stops and human cognitive work begins. It is not a vague commitment to responsible use — it is a specification: AI handles X, human handles Y, the gate is at Z.

production_viz.label: the phase gate: AI stops, human begins
production_viz.mechanic: a horizontal timeline with a vertical gate marker; left of gate: AI chip (slate); right of gate: HUMAN chip (teal); the gate is the control point; labeled 'AI handles X / human handles Y'

beats.narration_text: For the teacher: the cognitive work that constitutes teaching is lesson delivery — the relationship with students, the metacognitive supervision of their understanding. AI handles preparation: quiz drafts, reading level adjustments, source organization. The gate is at delivery. The teacher does more of the work that is teaching.

production_viz.label: teacher gate: AI before delivery, human at delivery
production_viz.mechanic: horizontal timeline with gate; left: AI handles 'QUIZ DRAFTS / SOURCES / FORMATTING' (slate chips); right: HUMAN handles 'LESSON DELIVERY / STUDENT RELATIONSHIP' (teal chips); teal on the right — where teaching lives

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] teacher phase gate — AI before, teaching after

beats.narration_text: For the student: the cognitive work that constitutes learning is the writing and reasoning itself — the argument, the synthesis, the revision. AI handling that work is not assistance. It is substitution. The brain never does the work that produces the capability.

card.copy: AI handling the learning work is substitution, not assistance.

beats.narration_text: The phase gate for the student is the inverse of the teacher's gate — but follows the same logic. AI can locate sources and build an outline. The writing stays human. Because the writing is where the learning lives.

production_viz.label: student gate: AI before writing, human at writing
production_viz.mechanic: horizontal timeline with gate; left: AI handles 'SOURCES / OUTLINE / FORMATTING' (slate chips); right: HUMAN handles 'ARGUMENT / SYNTHESIS / REVISION' (teal chips); the gate sits before the writing

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] student phase gate — AI before, writing after

beats.narration_text: Tariq uses AI to draft the essay, tweaks for style, submits in twenty minutes. Priya uses AI to locate sources and build an outline, then writes for ninety minutes herself. Both earn a B-plus. Three months later, Tariq cannot construct a historical argument from scratch. Priya can.

production_viz.label: Tariq and Priya: same grade, different capability
production_viz.mechanic: two columns (TARIQ / PRIYA); row 1: both get B+ (teal); row 2: 'THREE MONTHS LATER' — Tariq chip crimson 'CANNOT ARGUE', Priya chip teal 'CAN ARGUE'; gate position column labels the difference

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Tariq and Priya — same grade, different outcome

beats.narration_text: The grade was the same. The capability was not. The difference is not the tool — it is where the gate was placed relative to the cognitive work that constitutes the skill.

card.copy: The gate position determines whether AI amplifies or substitutes.

beats.narration_text: Some cognitive work is extraneous — formatting, organizing, drafting boilerplate — work that occupies mental bandwidth without building the capability you are after. Delegating that to AI frees capacity for the work that matters. Other cognitive work is germane: it IS the learning. Delegating it loses the learning.

production_viz.label: extraneous vs germane cognitive load
production_viz.mechanic: two rows: top 'EXTRANEOUS LOAD' (slate chip) labeled 'formatting / boilerplate / organizing' -> AI delegates (teal outcome); bottom 'GERMANE LOAD' (teal chip) labeled 'argument / synthesis / judgment' -> human keeps (teal outcome); wrong move: germane to AI (crimson outcome)

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] extraneous vs germane load — what to delegate

beats.narration_text: The practice move: before any task, identify where the learning lives in what you are trying to build. That is where the gate goes. AI handles everything before it. Human handles everything on the other side.

production_viz.label: gate placement: find where learning lives, set gate there
production_viz.mechanic: a task bar with a movable gate marker; a LEARNING LIVES HERE label marks a zone in teal; the gate is placed at the left edge of that zone; AI zone is gray left of gate, human zone is teal right of gate

beats.narration_text: For the student: AI locates primary sources and builds an outline. The student writes the argument for ninety minutes. For the teacher: AI drafts the quiz and adjusts reading levels. The teacher delivers the lesson. Both use the same tool. Both keep the cognitive work that builds their capability.
beats.scene_description: student writing by hand at a desk, surrounded by source materials and notes, focused and engaged; editorial newsprint desaturated treatment; slow push-in
beats.image_prompt: B13, student writing by hand at a desk surrounded by open books and printed source materials, focused engaged expression, editorial desaturated newsprint treatment, flat print lighting, no text legible, collage aesthetic
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] student doing the germane cognitive work

beats.narration_text: The check: before using AI for any task, ask — is this the cognitive work that builds the capability I want? If yes, keep it. If no, delegate it. The gate is not where you trust AI less. It is where the human cognitive work is irreplaceable.

production_viz.label: the gate check: does this build the capability?
production_viz.mechanic: a decision diamond: 'Does this build the capability I want?' -> YES (teal) -> 'KEEP IT / HUMAN DOES IT'; NO (slate) -> 'DELEGATE TO AI'; YES path highlighted in teal

beats.narration_text: The gate is not about trusting AI less. It is about identifying which cognitive work is irreplaceable — and keeping it. AI amplifies capability when it frees human attention for the work that matters. It substitutes when it takes over that work.

card.copy: The gate is where the human cognitive work is irreplaceable.

beats.narration_text: Same tool. Opposite outcome. Because the gate was in different places. The teacher who uses AI for preparation does more teaching. The student who lets AI do the writing does less learning.

card.copy: Gate position determines whether AI amplifies or replaces you.
